mission.title: keycloak: Persist session in JDBC store without using external infinispan cluster
mission.description: Persist session in JDBC store without using external infinispan cluster. Requested by 33+ users.

steps.title: Check current Keycloak setup
steps.description:
Verify your Keycloak version and current session persistence configuration:
```bash
# If running in a container:
docker exec keycloak /opt/keycloak/bin/kc.sh show-config

# Check if persistent-user-sessions feature is available:
docker exec keycloak /opt/keycloak/bin/kc.sh build --features=persistent-user-sessions
```
This feature is available in Keycloak 25+ and is enabled by default in Keycloak 26+.

steps.title: Understand the problem
steps.description:
By default, older Keycloak versions stored user sessions only in Infinispan caches. In a multi-node deployment, this required an external Infinispan cluster for session replication. Losing an Infinispan node could lead to session loss.

The persistent-user-sessions feature stores sessions in the database (JDBC), eliminating the need for an external Infinispan cluster while still maintaining session data across restarts.

steps.title: Enable persistent user sessions
steps.description:
Enable the persistent-user-sessions feature in Keycloak:
```bash
# Build Keycloak with the feature enabled:
/opt/keycloak/bin/kc.sh build --features=persistent-user-sessions

# Or set it via environment variable:
export KC_FEATURES=persistent-user-sessions

# Start Keycloak:
/opt/keycloak/bin/kc.sh start
```

In Keycloak 26+, this feature is enabled by default and no additional configuration is needed. See the [source PR](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/15619) for implementation details.

steps.title: Verify sessions persist across restarts
steps.description:
Test that sessions survive a Keycloak server restart:
1. Log in to Keycloak as a user to create an active session
2. Check active sessions in the Admin Console under **Sessions**
3. Restart the Keycloak container or pod
4. Verify that the user session is still active after restart

resolution.summary: Keycloak added the persistent-user-sessions feature which stores user sessions in the database (JDBC store) instead of relying solely on Infinispan caches. This eliminates the need for an external Infinispan cluster in multi-node deployments. The feature is available in Keycloak 25+ and enabled by default in Keycloak 26+.

prerequisites.description: A running Keycloak server (via Docker/Podman or Kubernetes with the Keycloak Operator) with database connectivity.
